findings.description: Table title formatting is altered in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the title appears as a compact centered table caption in uppercase directly above the table. In the HTML, the same title is enlarged, left-aligned, broken across multiple lines, and separated by a horizontal rule, making it visually distinct from the original presentation.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated styling/branding text not present in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML <style> contains comment/text and identifiers such as "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", font imports, and CSS variables like "--nc-font", "--nc-navy", "--nc-blue". These are not part of the PDF page content, which is a plain scanned table headed "TABLE 74. COUNTY-WIDE TAX LEVY COMPONENTS EXPRESSED AS A PERCENTAGE BY TYPE OF PROPERTY BY COUNTY, FISCAL YEAR 2014-2015".

findings.description: A table header unit is incorrect in the HTML: the final column on the right half is marked with dollars instead of percent.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, both left and right table groups label the last column as "Public Service Company Property:" with percentages shown in the cells. In the HTML header comment/thead, the last right-side column is "Public Service Company Property [$]", while the actual values are percentages such as "2.26%", "5.07%", and "2.86%".
